home.metaTitle: Learn AI for free. Donate to the cause.
home.lede: From your first prompt to advanced engineering — a serious, clear, free guide. If it helps you, every donation goes entirely to NGOs that bring technology to those who lack access. The money never passes through us.
home.impactLabel: Total donated to the cause — passed through in full
home.impactSub: Updated in real time. Every cent goes to digital-inclusion partner NGOs.
home.pathsTitle: The same site, three depths. Move at your own pace.
home.p1desc: What a prompt is, why form matters, and how to get useful answers on the first try.
home.p2desc: Structure requests, give context and examples, and build reliable everyday workflows.
home.p3desc: Chain-of-thought, tools, evaluation, security, and production patterns.
home.teachTitle: Every technique comes with the right way and the wrong way.
home.teachLede: No empty theory. You see the prompt, the why, and the contrast side by side.
home.teachGood: Rewrite in formal tone, max 3 sentences, keeping the numbers.
home.whyLema: AI is the new literacy. And literacy isn't for sale.
home.whyBody: Knowing how to use AI has become literacy. And literacy isn't for sale. Charging for it only deepens the inequality we want to fight. So the knowledge stays open and those who can give back fund digital inclusion for people who haven't even come online yet.

aprender.crumb: The anatomy of a good prompt
aprender.title: The anatomy of a good prompt
aprender.lede: A prompt isn't a loose question — it's an instruction. The clearer the instruction, the better the answer. There's a simple recipe that works almost every time.
aprender.h2recipe: The recipe: role · task · context · format
aprender.pRecipe: Think of asking something of a very capable colleague who just arrived: they do exactly what you say, but don't guess what you want. Tell them who they are, what to do, with what material, and in what format.
aprender.pcL2: Write an email politely declining the invitation below, citing schedule.
aprender.pcKey: Max 4 sentences. Cordial tone. No emojis.
aprender.pContrast: The same request, vague and specific. The difference isn't in the model — it's in you.
aprender.good: Write an email declining the invitation, in a cordial tone, max 4 sentences, citing schedule as the reason.
aprender.bad: reply to this invite for me
aprender.pWhy: The good request removes ambiguity in three points: the action (decline), the constraints (tone, length) and the reason (schedule). The bad request lets the model guess — and it will guess wrong often.
aprender.pPractice: Take a request you'd make to AI today and rewrite it using the recipe. Compare the two answers. The improvement is usually immediate.

causa.h1: The money never passes through us.
causa.lede: Every donation goes straight to the partner NGOs' accounts. We don't touch it, don't keep a fee, don't pay salaries with it. Here's the proof, line by line.
causa.flowTitle: Three steps. Zero retention in between.
causa.s1desc: Via PIX or card, straight to the partner NGO's checkout.
causa.s2desc: The destination account is the institution's, not ours. We don't touch the amount.
causa.s3desc: Every transfer appears in the open ledger below, with date and destination.
causa.ledgerSub: Inflows in grey, transfers in green. Balance kept by us: always R$ 0.00.
causa.partnersTitle: Who takes the technology to the front line.
causa.p1desc: AI labs in public schools on the outskirts of São Paulo.
causa.p2desc: Internet and equipment for riverside communities in Pará.
causa.p3desc: AI training for youth and adults in inland Bahia.
causa.ledgerEmpty: No transfers recorded yet — they will appear here as partner NGOs report them.

doar.lede: Pick an amount. It goes entirely to a partner NGO — you get the transfer receipt.
doar.impact20: Learning material on AI for one student.
doar.impact50: One hour of an AI workshop in a public school.
doar.impact100: A class connected for a week.
doar.impact250: A connectivity kit for a community.
doar.impactBase: Every cent brings someone closer to technology.

manifesto.h1: AI is the new literacy. And literacy isn't for sale.
manifesto.lede: Knowing how to use AI has become literacy. And literacy isn't for sale. Charging for it only deepens the inequality we want to fight. So the knowledge stays open and those who can give back fund digital inclusion for people who haven't even come online yet.
manifesto.storyTitle: It started with an account that didn't add up.
manifesto.story1: On one side, AI became the most powerful tool an ordinary person has ever held. On the other, the knowledge of how to use it well stayed locked away: expensive courses, shallow forums, threads that promise "hacks" and deliver noise. Those who need it most are exactly those who reach it least.
manifesto.story2: PromptCause was born to undo that knot. We gathered, in one place, a serious and free guide — from your first prompt to advanced engineering — with no paywall, no ads, no mandatory login. And we gave it a purpose beyond teaching: turning gratitude into digital inclusion.
manifesto.story3: Because it's not enough to teach AI to those who already have a computer, internet and time. The next person to learn may be someone who hasn't even reached the network yet. It's for them that every donation goes.
manifesto.pr1body: No paywall, no ads, no mandatory login. Learning to use AI is a right, not a product. Use, translate and share freely.
manifesto.pr2title: The money never passes through us
manifesto.pr2body: Every donation goes straight to the partner NGOs' accounts. We keep R$ 0.00 — and we prove it, line by line.
manifesto.pr3body: No "secret hack" or promise of a magic shortcut. We show the right way and the wrong way, side by side, and always explain why.
manifesto.pr4body: Multilingual content on principle. Inclusion starts with understanding — so anyone can read PromptCause in their own language.
manifesto.promiseQuote: “Free. If it helped you, 100% of the donation goes to the cause.”
manifesto.promiseBody: PromptCause is run by volunteers. The site's costs come out of our own pocket, not the donations — that's why the counter can honestly show 100% passed through.
manifesto.closingBody: Start for free today. If one day this opens a door for you, help open the next one for someone else.

mim-social.descricao: Education through technology: literacy and digital inclusion for children and teenagers in the Nestlé favela, Jaboatão dos Guararapes (PE), Brazil.

jovens-hackers.descricao: A coding, robotics and maker-culture school by and for the outskirts. Over a thousand students since 2017.
